_about: Labeled golden corpus for the gitlogs commit-intelligence classifier. Each entry is a fictional, NON-PII commit with a human label ('worthy' = should be tweeted, 'noise' = should be skipped) and a one-line note explaining the call. This file is the SPEC for 'good triage': the eval harness scores src/commitIntelligence.js against these labels. Keep labels honest and defensible; do not edit labels to make a buggy classifier pass.

commits.message: feat(api): add cursor pagination to /users endpoint
commits.label: worthy
commits.note: Substantive user-facing feature touching source with a descriptive subject.

commits.message: fix(auth): reject expired JWT before DB lookup
commits.label: worthy
commits.note: Real bug fix in auth path; clear behavioral change worth announcing.

commits.message: perf(db): batch ledger writes, p99 820ms to 140ms
commits.label: worthy
commits.note: Measurable performance win with concrete numbers — prime tweet material.

commits.message: feat(billing): support proration on mid-cycle plan changes
commits.label: worthy
commits.note: New billing capability with multiple source files; clearly newsworthy.

commits.message: fix(checkout): prevent double-charge on rapid resubmit
commits.label: worthy
commits.note: High-impact correctness fix; descriptive and source-touching.

commits.message: feat(api)!: drop deprecated v1 token endpoints
commits.label: worthy
commits.note: Breaking change (!) — exactly the kind of news consumers must hear about.

commits.message: perf(search): add trigram index, cut query time 60%
commits.label: worthy
commits.note: Quantified perf improvement touching source.

commits.message: fix(upload): stream large files instead of buffering in memory
commits.label: worthy
commits.note: Real reliability fix; descriptive subject, source files.

commits.message: feat(webhooks): add HMAC signature verification
commits.label: worthy
commits.note: Security feature; clear and substantive.

commits.message: refactor(payments): extract retry policy into a reusable module
commits.label: worthy
commits.note: Borderline: refactors rarely make news, but this is a descriptive, source-touching, focused change. Calling it worthy because the scorer (refactor base 30 + source 12 + focused 6 + descriptive 8 + scope 3 = 59) treats it as substantive engineering work and it crosses the bar.

commits.message: revert: undo the aggressive cache TTL change from 9f8e7d6
commits.label: worthy
commits.note: Borderline: a revert of a user-visible change is itself news ('we rolled it back'). Scorer: revert base 40 + source 12 + focused 6 = 58.

commits.message: feat(cli): add --watch mode to the dev server command
commits.label: worthy
commits.note: Developer-facing feature; descriptive and source-touching.

commits.message: chore(deps): bump lockfile after dependency update
commits.label: noise
commits.note: Lockfile-only dependency bump — classic spam, should be skipped.

commits.message: build(deps): update package-lock.json
commits.label: noise
commits.note: Another lockfile-only bump; no human-readable change.

commits.message: Merge branch 'main' into feature/new-dashboard
commits.label: noise
commits.note: Merge commit — not original news even though it lists source files.

commits.message: wip
commits.label: noise
commits.note: Work-in-progress scratch commit; vague + wip signal.

commits.message: fixup! tweak the thing
commits.label: noise
commits.note: fixup! squash commit — internal noise, not news.

commits.label: noise
commits.note: Bare version tag commit touching only package.json.

commits.message: chore(release): bump version to 2.0.0
commits.label: noise
commits.note: Version bump / release commit; no substantive change.

commits.message: update
commits.label: noise
commits.note: Vague one-word subject; uninformative for a changelog.

commits.message: changes
commits.label: noise
commits.note: Vague subject; no signal about what actually changed.

commits.message: style: run prettier across the repo
commits.label: noise
commits.note: Formatting-only change; cosmetic + style base, not newsworthy.

commits.message: docs: fix typo in README
commits.label: noise
commits.note: Typo-only doc fix; cosmetic, no product news.

commits.message: chore(assets): replace logo and favicon
commits.label: noise
commits.note: Assets-only change; no behavioral/news content.

commits.message: ci: cache pnpm store in GitHub Actions
commits.label: noise
commits.note: CI plumbing; internal tooling, not user-facing news.

commits.message: test: add coverage for the cursor pagination helper
commits.label: noise
commits.note: Test-only change; low base, not announcement-worthy on its own.

commits.message: docs: rewrite the entire architecture guide with new diagrams
commits.label: noise
commits.note: Genuinely borderline. A major docs overhaul COULD be worth a tweet, but per the project's triage stance docs are background work and the subject names no product change. Labeling noise: docs base 22 + descriptive 8 + focused 6 = ~36, below the 40 bar — and that matches the editorial judgment that a doc rewrite is not product news.

commits.message: feat: stuff
commits.label: noise
commits.note: Borderline trap: real source change but a useless subject. The triage rule is that a tweet needs a describable change; 'stuff' gives the generator nothing. Scorer: feat 60 - vague 22 + source 12 + focused 6 = 56, so the classifier will likely call it WORTHY. Labeled noise on editorial grounds — this is an expected hard case / possible classifier gap.

commits.message: refactor: rename internal variables for clarity
commits.label: noise
commits.note: Internal-only refactor with no external behavior change. Scorer: refactor 30 + source 12 + focused 6 + descriptive 8 = 56 -> likely WORTHY. Labeled noise editorially; another expected hard case.

commits.message: feat(notifications): add email digest with daily/weekly cadence
commits.label: worthy
commits.note: Clear user-facing feature; descriptive, multiple source files.
